Claim: One statement about acute leukemia in children is false.
Evidence:
- Acute lymphoblastic leukemia (ALL) is more prevalent than acute myeloid leukemia (AML) in children.
- Statistical data consistently show higher incidence rates for ALL compared to AML in pediatric populations.
